title: Mail Clerks and Mail Machine Operators, Except Postal Service
description: Prepare incoming and outgoing mail for distribution. Time-stamp, open, read, sort, and route incoming mail; and address, seal, stamp, fold, stuff, and affix postage to outgoing mail or packages. Duties may also include keeping necessary records and completed forms.

tasks.text: Wrap packages or bundles by hand, or by using tying machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Verify that items are addressed correctly, marked with the proper postage, and in suitable condition for processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove containers of sorted mail or parcels and transfer them to designated areas according to established procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Sort and route incoming mail, and collect outgoing mail, using carts as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Affix postage to packages or letters by hand, or stamp materials, using postage meters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine manner in which mail is to be sent, and prepare it for delivery to mailing facilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Accept and check containers of mail or parcels from large volume mailers, couriers, and contractors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Seal or open envelopes, by hand or by using machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Weigh packages or letters to determine postage needed, using weighing scales and rate charts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect mail machine output for defects and determine how to eliminate causes of any defects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Remove from machines printed materials, such as labeled articles, postmarked envelopes or tape, and folded sheets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Release packages or letters to customers upon presentation of written notices or other identification.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate computer-controlled keyboards or voice recognition equipment to direct items according to established routing schemes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Answer inquiries regarding shipping or mailing policies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lift and unload containers of mail or parcels onto equipment for transportation to sortation stations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Contact delivery or courier services to arrange delivery of letters and parcels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place incoming or outgoing letters or packages into sacks or bins based on destination or type, and place identifying tags on sacks or bins.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clear jams in sortation equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mail merchandise samples or promotional literature in response to requests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust guides, rollers, loose card inserters, weighing machines, and tying arms, using rules and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read production orders to determine types and sizes of items scheduled for printing and mailing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sell mail products, and accept payment for products and mailing charges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start machines that automatically feed plates, stencils, or tapes through mechanisms, and observe machine operations to detect any malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Stamp dates and times of receipt of incoming mail.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Add ink, fill paste reservoirs, and change machine ribbons when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fold letters or circulars and insert them in envelopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
